title: Payback Period
event: Reyes Co. considers a $240,000 machine expected to generate $60,000 of net cash flow per year. Compute the payback period.

computationPaths.narration: Payback asks one blunt question: how fast do I get my money back?

memorize.body: Payback = investment ÷ annual net CASH flow (even flows). Uneven flows: accumulate year by year until recovered.
Payback uses CASH flow, not net income — and it ignores both the time value of money and everything after recovery.

questions.prompt: Investment $240,000, annual net cash flow $60,000. Payback period (years) = ?

distractors.feedback: You likely divided by net INCOME (cash flow minus depreciation). Payback runs on CASH: 240 ÷ 60 = 4 years.
Inverted — that's the payback RECIPROCAL. Investment over annual cash flow: 4 years.
